mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations. A 1-month decline in FEDFUNDS produces positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days through mechanical repricing of equity risk premiums.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in < 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration and recession risk, triggering equity risk-off positioning. When UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, S&P 500 experiences negative 20-day forward returns as investors reduce equity exposure.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.1% suggests tight labor market
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession signal
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in < 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) reflects consumer spending strength and aggregate demand. Sustained month-over-month growth in RSAFS signals robust economic activity and corporate earnings expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised upward.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 492003.0M indicates consumer spending level
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with sustained consumer demand
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in < 52% of periods with month-over-month RSAFS growth, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and employment (PAYEMS) jointly signal supply-side capacity utilization. When both INDPRO and PAYEMS grow simultaneously, firms operate at high capacity with pricing power, supporting equity valuations. This interaction effect produces positive 5-day returns as market reprices growth expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.24 and PAYEMS at 147810K indicate production and employment levels
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in < 53% of instances where both INDPRO and PAYEMS grow month-over-month, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and real GDP (GDPC1) jointly capture demand-side momentum. When HOUST accelerates while GDPC1 remains elevated, construction activity and capital investment drive forward earnings growth. This interaction produces positive 60-day returns through multiplier effects on corporate revenues.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1192K and GDPC1 at 17272.5B indicate construction and output levels
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in < 51% of instances where HOUST growth exceeds 2% month-over-month and GDPC1 is above trend, or interaction term is not significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 3% combined with FEDFUNDS below 2% signals real negative rates, eroding bond returns and forcing capital into equities. This regime produces positive 20-day returns as investors rebalance from fixed income to equities seeking real return preservation.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 249.25 and FEDFUNDS at 1.41% indicate near-negative real rates
regime_hint: financial repression regime with negative real rates
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in < 54% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100K per month while UNRATE remains stable, signaling labor market softening without immediate recession. This threshold condition triggers equity repricing downward over 20 days as forward earnings growth expectations are revised lower due to reduced consumer income growth.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 100K AND UNRATE unchanged or rising
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 147810K and UNRATE at 4.1% provide baseline
regime_hint: labor market deceleration without full recession
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in < 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while CPIAUCSL remains elevated, signaling stagflationary pressure. This regime produces negative 60-day returns as equity risk premiums expand due to margin compression from rising input costs and falling output.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.24 and CPIAUCSL at 249.25 indicate production and price levels
regime_hint: stagflationary regime with supply constraints and persistent inflation
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in < 52% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between INDPRO decline and CPIAUCSL elevation is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
